title: rhododendron calendulaceum
intro: Rhododendron calendulaceum, also known as the flame azalea, is a deciduous shrub native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. Rhododendron calendulaceum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and skin irritations.
